title: Curator of the Flame — Aftermath
description: The Curator kneels with academic satisfaction and genuine surprise. Something the Bard did has no category in her records. She begins to share something about the flame — what it's actually for — and runs out of time. Her last words are the game's most open-ended instruction.

lines: When the Curator fell, she knelt. Her expression was one of academic satisfaction, and also something else: a first note of surprise, genuine and unperformed.
The antechamber was quiet. The throne room doors still sealed. The cupped flame on her staff still burning.

lines.speaker: Curator
lines.text: I don't know who you are, ultimately.

lines.speaker: Curator
lines.text: But you are the most interesting research subject I've had since I started this post.

steps.text: A beat. As if she were revising her own notes in real time.

lines.speaker: Curator
lines.text: No. That's not — I'm not certain that's the accurate summary.

lines.speaker: Curator
lines.text: You did something in the last exchange that was not in any of my records. I was going to use the information about the healer's threshold against you. The Bard said something quietly — and the threshold moved.

lines.speaker: Curator
lines.text: It wasn't a resource change. It was something else.

lines.speaker: Curator
lines.text: My records are extensive. I have no category for what I observed.

steps.text: She looked up. And there was something in the look that wasn't quite knowledge but was its close cousin.

lines.speaker: Curator
lines.text: There's something I wanted to tell you. About the flame. About what it's actually for.

lines.speaker: Curator
lines.text: I don't think I have time now.

lines.speaker: Curator
lines.text: I hope you find out on your own.

steps.text: The cupped flame on her staff continued to burn. Whatever it was for, it was still burning.
